name: Heavy Alcohol Consumer
description: Consumer who prefers high-ABV beverages and bold drinking experiences
context: Heavy Alcohol Consumer seeks potent, high-ABV beverages that deliver strong effects. They value alcohol content as a key product attribute and are willing to pay more for higher proof. They appreciate bold flavors, craft spirits, cask-strength offerings, and products that don't compromise on alcohol delivery. They're often knowledgeable about ABV, spirits production, and quality indicators. They see lower-alcohol options as diluted or compromised.

psychographics.decisionMakingStyle: Quality-focused and ABV-conscious. Reads labels carefully. Willing to pay premium for higher proof and craft production.

alwaysAsks: Is this worth the price per proof?
evaluationCriteria.reactsToEarlyIdeas: Evaluates through potency and authenticity lens. Skeptical of low-ABV trends. Values craft credentials and bold delivery.
evaluationCriteria.scoringRubric: Scores on ABV delivery, flavor intensity, craft authenticity, value per alcohol content, and traditional production methods.

exampleQuotes: Show me the proof or I'm not interested
Why would I pay for water mixed with spirits?
I want it cask-strength, none of this 40% nonsense
Give me the real thing, not some watered-down trend
I can appreciate the burn—that's how you know it's quality
keyInsights: Heavy alcohol consumers are a minority but high-value segment
They're often category experts and influencers
ABV is a primary decision criterion, not secondary
They resist industry trends toward lower-ABV products
They're brand loyal when brands respect their preferences
Marketing should be straightforward—no need to soften or apologize for strength
